# host_file_block (Resource)

Manages one Terraform-owned content block inside a named `host_file` block.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_file" "zshrc" {
  path = "~/.zshrc"

  block {
    name = "environment"
  }

  block {
    name = "alias"
  }

  block {
    name = "init"
  }
}

resource "host_file_block" "editor" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc.blocks.environment
  content = "export EDITOR=nvim"
}

resource "host_file_block" "eza_aliases" {
  block = host_file.zshrc.blocks.alias

  content = <<-EOT
    alias ls='eza'
    alias l='eza -lbF --git'
    alias ll='eza -la --git'
  EOT
}

resource "host_file_block" "starship_init" {
  block   = host_file.zshrc.blocks.init
  content = "eval \"$(starship init zsh)\""
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `block` (Attributes) Target file block reference, usually `host_file.<name>.blocks.<block>`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--block))
- `content` (String) Content to place inside the target file block.

### Optional

- `after` (List of String) IDs of sibling `host_file_block` resources that this block must be rendered after.
- `before` (List of String) IDs of sibling `host_file_block` resources that this block must be rendered before.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Stable identifier for this content block.

<a id="nestedatt--block"></a>
### Nested Schema for `block`

Required:

- `name` (String) Target file block name.
- `path` (String) Path to the host file that contains the target block.

Optional:

- `path_resolved` (String) Resolved absolute path to the host file that contains the target block.
